summary.notes: Walked content/marines (221 mdx), content/leader (88 mdx), content/commander (46 mdx), content/admin (386 mdx), content/citations (310 mdx, _stubs excluded), content/tools (6 mdx), content/videos (347 mdx), content/inspections (42 program JSON), 30 per-role page.tsx and layout.tsx files, src/lib/role-trees.ts, src/lib/roles.ts, src/lib/content/loader.ts, src/components/shell (top-nav, side-nav, tree-nav, role-picker-dialog, command-palette, role-route-sync), src/lib/store/role-store.ts, src/components/domain/citations-browser.tsx, and the four src/generated role catalogs (marines, leader, commander, admin). The 10 inspection _policy-indexes JSON files lack roles arrays by design. Loader walks only INSPECTION_TRACKS dirs (igmc, mcaat), so support manifests are not flagged.

findings.message: Command palette renders a 'Switch role' group that calls setRole on selection. Per CLAUDE.md Section 2.2 the canonical role-switching surfaces are top-nav.tsx (desktop) and role-picker-dialog.tsx (mobile only). A third interactive switcher in command-palette violates the single-switcher rule.
findings.fix: Remove the 'Switch role' Command.Group from command-palette and route role-change actions to the role-picker-dialog. The palette routes to roles, it does not set them.

findings.message: Soft-deleted .trash- file under content/marines. Loader skips dotfiles, but the file remains in the working tree and ships in the repo.
findings.fix: Delete the file from the repo.

findings.message: RoleRouteSync calls setRole from a pathname effect to keep the 'Viewing as' label in sync with the URL. This is route-driven, not interactive UI, so it is not a second user-facing switcher, but it does write to the role store from a third surface. Worth a documentation note in the role-store comment block.
findings.fix: Add a comment to role-store and to role-route-sync explaining the route-derived write path so the single-switcher rule is unambiguous.

findings.snippet: Search dominant at center. Segmented role switcher is the only role-switching surface.
findings.message: Top-nav doc comment claims a segmented role switcher lives in this component, but no role switcher renders here. The actual canonical switcher lives in role-picker-dialog (mobile drawer). Mismatch between Section 3.1 mockup expectations and current shipped UI.
findings.fix: Either restore the segmented role control in top-nav per Section 3.1 chrome spec, or update the comment to point at role-picker-dialog as the canonical switcher.
